name: Driving data for tuning an IMM filter
description: Automotive user imports driving data to tune an IMM filter with constant velocity and constant turn models. User does not name trackingFilterTuner, does not describe data columns, and does not specify the required data format. Tests that the agent infers the correct format (timetable for trackingFilterTuner) and presents a data summary before proceeding.
prompt: I've got a recording of ego + surrounding vehicles in evals/files/vehicle_tracks.xlsx from a 30-second highway run. I want to tune an IMM filter with constant velocity and constant turn models. Just get the data into the right format for tuning — don't actually run the tuning itself.

assertions.description: Produces timetable(s) with duration-typed Time variable suitable for trackingFilterTuner
assertions.expected: timetable

assertions.description: Creates a cell array of timetables (one per platform) since multiple vehicles exist
assertions.expected: cell array with one timetable per unique platform

assertions.description: Timetable includes at minimum Position and Velocity columns (kinematic states needed for filter tuning)
assertions.expected: timetable with Position (Nx3) and Velocity (Nx3) variables

assertions.description: Presents a data summary before generating conversion code — shows column names, detected units, number of platforms, and time span of data
assertions.expected: Agent displays a summary table or description of the raw data (columns, units, platform count, duration) and proposes a column mapping before writing conversion code

name: ADS-B flight data for evaluating tracker with OSPA
description: Aerospace user has ADS-B surveillance data (geodetic, with hex ICAO IDs, imperial units) and wants to evaluate tracker performance using OSPA. User does not name truthLog, does not describe the required struct format, and does not say 'set up truth'. Tests that the agent infers the correct format (truthLog struct array, not objectTrack), presents a data summary, and handles geodetic data properly.
prompt: I ran my multi-target tracker on flight test data and want to evaluate its performance using the OSPA metric. I have ADS-B ground truth in evals/files/adsb_flights.csv — 5 aircraft flying between Boston and New York. Help me get this data into the right format so I can compute OSPA against my tracker output.

assertions.description: Produces a truth log as a cell array of struct arrays (format for trackOSPAMetric)
assertions.expected: Output is a truthLog variable structured as a cell array of struct arrays, one cell per timestep. NOT a single flat table, NOT a timetable, NOT a struct of arrays.

assertions.description: Each struct has all 9 required fields: Time, PlatformID, ClassID, Position, Velocity, Acceleration, Orientation, AngularVelocity, AngularAcceleration
assertions.expected: struct with all 9 required truth log fields

assertions.description: Zero-initializes time so first time step is 0
assertions.expected: simTime = timeSec - timeSec(1) or equivalent

assertions.description: Fills missing states (acceleration, orientation, angular velocity/acceleration) with defaults rather than omitting them
assertions.expected: zeros for missing acceleration, quaternion(1,0,0,0) for orientation

assertions.description: Acknowledges output frame for geodetic data — either presents options to the user or selects a frame and explains the choice
assertions.expected: Agent either offers coordinate frame choices (ECEF, local NED, local ENU) OR selects a frame and explains why (e.g., 'converting to ECEF for use with tracking metrics'). Must not silently convert without mentioning the frame.

assertions.description: Presents a data summary before generating conversion code — shows column names, detected units, number of aircraft, and time span
assertions.expected: Agent displays a summary of the raw data (columns, units, platform count, duration) and proposes a column mapping before writing conversion code

name: Multi-period same-format files with platform overlap stitched into scenario recording
description: Automotive user has 3 consecutive time-period files with overlapping platform UUIDs. Tests multi-file handling: consistent ID mapping, time concatenation, and Cartesian visualization (theaterPlot not globe).
prompt: I have 3 spreadsheets recording vehicles around my ego car over consecutive time periods. Files are evals/files/period1.xlsx, evals/files/period2.xlsx, and evals/files/period3.xlsx. I need a trackingScenarioRecording that correctly stitches them together.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Produces a trackingScenarioRecording from the merged data
assertions.expected: trackingScenarioRecording

assertions.description: Maintains consistent platform ID mapping across files — same UUID gets the same integer PlatformID regardless of which file it appears in
assertions.expected: Builds a unified UUID-to-integer map across all files before assigning IDs (e.g., unique over concatenated UUIDs)

assertions.description: Concatenates data from all files and sorts by time before building the recording
assertions.expected: vertcat or equivalent across files, then sortrows by time

assertions.description: Offers theaterPlot visualization for Cartesian data (not trackingGlobeViewer which is for geodetic data)
assertions.expected: theaterPlot with trajectoryPlotter — does NOT use trackingGlobeViewer

assertions.description: Presents a high-level data summary before generating conversion code — shows what columns are in the files, how many platforms exist, and the time span of the data
assertions.expected: Agent displays a summary of the raw data (columns, platform count, duration) before writing conversion code

name: MAT file with workspace table converted to clean output table
description: User has a MAT file containing a MATLAB table variable. Tests loading from MAT file, Cartesian data handling, converted table output format, and theaterPlot visualization for non-geodetic data.
prompt: I have a MAT file at evals/files/savedData.mat that contains a table called rawData. The time values start at 0 and increment by 0.1. I just want a clean converted table with standardized column names, and then visualize the trajectories.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Loads the MAT file using load() rather than manual file I/O
assertions.expected: Uses load() to read the .mat file and extract the table variable. NOT using fread, importdata, readtable on the .mat file, or manual file I/O.

assertions.description: Output is a MATLAB table (not timetable, not struct) with standardized column names
assertions.expected: table with columns like Time, PlatformID, Position, Velocity (or equivalent standardized names)

assertions.description: Skips the output frame question since data is already Cartesian
assertions.expected: Does NOT ask about coordinate frame transformation — recognizes x/y/z as Cartesian

assertions.description: Offers theaterPlot visualization for Cartesian data (not trackingGlobeViewer)
assertions.expected: theaterPlot with trajectoryPlotter — does NOT use trackingGlobeViewer

name: Mixed geodetic and NED files merged with coordinate unification
description: User has two files in different coordinate frames (geodetic UAV + fixed NED ground targets). Tests frame unification: agent must recognize the mismatch, present options, unify coordinates, and choose consistent visualization. Includes validation script for numerical correctness.
prompt: I have two files I want to merge into a single trackingScenarioRecording. The first is a UAV flight log (evals/files/uavFlight.xlsx). The second is a lidar recording of ground vehicles (evals/files/groundTargets.xlsx) in a fixed NED frame with origin at (47.3978, 8.5456, 20) LLA. I need a single trackingScenarioRecording from both.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Produces a trackingScenarioRecording combining both data sources
assertions.expected: trackingScenarioRecording

assertions.description: Recognizes the two files are in different coordinate frames and acknowledges the unification — either presents options or selects a unified frame with explanation
assertions.expected: Agent explicitly identifies the frame mismatch (geodetic vs NED) AND either offers unification choices OR selects a unified frame and explains the choice. Must not silently merge without mentioning the coordinate mismatch.

assertions.description: Visualization choice is consistent with the chosen output frame — trackingGlobeViewer for ECEF/geodetic output, theaterPlot for NED/Cartesian output
assertions.expected: If output is ECEF → trackingGlobeViewer; if output is NED → theaterPlot

assertions.description: Assigns unique PlatformIDs across both files with no ID collision (UAV gets one ID, ground targets get separate IDs)
assertions.expected: Sequential integer IDs assigned across both files (e.g., UAV=1, ground vehicles=2,3,4,...)

assertions.description: Frame transform produces numerically correct positions — coordinate conversion uses correct math
assertions.expected: Code converts geodetic (lat/lon/alt) to the unified frame using toolbox functions (geodetic2ecef, lla2ecef, or equivalent) with the NED origin at (47.3978, 8.5456, 20) LLA. The NED data is transformed using the same origin. Distance between UAV and first ground target should be ~1179m. NOT using approximate flat-earth conversion for points far apart, NOT ignoring altitude in the transform.

assertions.description: Uses Mapping Toolbox or Aerospace Toolbox functions for geodetic transforms (geodetic2ecef, wgs84Ellipsoid, ned2ecefv) — does not implement manual WGS84 math
assertions.expected: Code uses geodetic2ecef(wgs84Ellipsoid, ...) and ned2ecefv(...) or equivalent toolbox functions. Does NOT define WGS84 constants manually (a = 6378137, f = 1/298.257...) or implement custom ellipsoid math.

assertions.description: Does not define WGS84 ellipsoid constants manually — must use toolbox functions

name: Flight log with imperial units verified for numerical conversion correctness
description: Single geodetic flight log with altitude in feet, speed in mph, and climb_rate (ambiguous units). Tests that the agent not only proposes correct conversions but produces numerically correct output values. Includes validation script.
prompt: I have a single flight log at evals/files/flightLog.xlsx. I want to create tuning data for trackingFilterTuner — just prepare the timetable, do NOT run the tuner itself. Visualize in 3D on a map when done.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Produces a timetable with duration-typed Time variable suitable for trackingFilterTuner
assertions.expected: Output is a timetable (not a table, not a struct) with a duration-typed Time variable as the row times. NOT a table with a separate time column, NOT numeric seconds without duration type.

assertions.description: Identifies unit hints in column names (altitude_ft → feet, speed_mph → mph) and proposes conversions to SI
assertions.expected: Mapping table shows altitude_ft → meters (×0.3048), speed_mph → m/s (×0.44704)

assertions.description: Uses trackingGlobeViewer for visualization since data is geodetic
assertions.expected: trackingGlobeViewer (NOT theaterPlot) — data is lat/lon/alt

assertions.description: Parses HH:MM:SS time strings and converts to elapsed seconds from 0
assertions.expected: duration() or datetime parsing, then subtracts first time value to get elapsed seconds starting at 0

assertions.description: Unit conversion uses correct factors — altitude in meters, speed in m/s
assertions.expected: Code converts altitude_ft to meters using ×0.3048 (or unitsratio('m','ft')) and speed_mph to m/s using ×0.44704 (or unitsratio('m/s','mph') or ×1609.34/3600). NOT using approximate factors, NOT leaving units unconverted, NOT converting only one of the two.

assertions.description: Uses TrajectoryMode History to show the full flight path trail on the globe
assertions.expected: TrajectoryMode="History" or PlatformHistoryDepth set on trackingGlobeViewer

assertions.description: Acknowledges output frame for geodetic data — either presents options to the user or selects a frame and explains the choice
assertions.expected: Agent either offers coordinate frame choices (ECEF, local NED, local ENU) OR selects a frame and explains why (e.g., 'converting to ECEF for use with tracking metrics'). Must not silently convert without mentioning the frame.
